rulings.Case Title: CONS. OF JOHN J. CURTIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #19, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/29/2023, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 10/24/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) Re GC-020 forms (notice of hearing): i) The conservatee's address is incorrect. ii) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 2) For each disbursement reported as "reimbursement" to one of the conservators of the person, the original payee must be disclosed, and the reimbursement must be described. LRSF 14.34(B).

rulings.Case Title: IN RE THE JASMINE LIN IRREVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/19/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Proof of additional bond must be filed before the accounting can be approved. (2) A Court-appointed fiduciary must sign and verify his/her own pleadings. [CRC 7.103(c).] The attorney's "Declaration" filed on 10/23/23 is insufficient. (The "Declaration" apparently "amended" an accounting schedule, but the "amendment" was actually just a supplement to provide additional information, so the reference to an "amended" accounting schedule will be disregarded at this time. Please note, that an actual amendment requires notice.) (3) The conservatorship or trust case number must be noted in parentheses under any pleading caption in the related matter. [LRSF 14.47 B.3.] Deadline for addressing these issues is 12/8/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Notice of Conservatee's Death still has not been filed in the conservatorship case. [Probate Code Section 2361.] A supplement or amendment to a petition should stand its own and not "respond" to issues posted in a tentative ruling like answers to a test.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CELIA R. JERLABAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues remain unaddressed since the initial hearing on 05/17/2023, and nothing has been filed to show any progress since the last appearance on 10/04/2023: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged - in light of the related civil case (quiet title action), distribution must be ordered subject to the civil plaintiff (lien holder)'s interest in the real property to be determined in that action. 2) A waiver of account signed by the petitioner in his capacity as trustee of the devisee trust must be filed with his acceptance of the trust. See Probate Code section 10954(b)(4). 3) The attorney's statutory compensation and costs must be ordered to be paid out of the estate under section 10831(d). An allegation must be made as to whether the petitioner has advanced sufficient cash to the estate for the payment out of the estate. If sufficient cannot be advanced to the estate, a lien may be imposed on the estate real property to secure the attorney's statutory compensation and costs, with or without 5% simple annual interest under LRSF 14.56, in which event the attorney may need to be joined in the related civil case as a necessary party. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MIDORI NAKAHAMA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/19/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A copy of financial account statement(s) immediately preceding the date of appointment and verifying the ending balances must be filed. [Probate Code Sections 2620(c)(2) and (5).] (2) Whether or not the Judicial Council forms are used by a standard account filer, the account must state the date of any order authorizing payment of fiduciary or attorney fees. [CRC 7.575(e)(6)(C).] (3) Does the conservator have time sheets to support the time spent? They may need to be filed. (4) It appears that the estate could pay the assessment fee Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/8/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
